description: Fixed cleaning method definitions for SSA Layer Spine. Includes natural and culture-specific methods with default steps, cues, safety notes, and variants.

general: Ventilate when using sprays, alcohol, peroxide, essential oils, or strong fragrances.
Label all DIY solutions and store away from children/pets.
Test any solution on an inconspicuous spot first.
Avoid saturating electrical components; unplug before cleaning.

notes: Focus on visible surfaces and pathways. Stop at the timer.

defaultSteps.title: Start timer
defaultSteps.action: Set a 5-minute timer and choose one room.

defaultSteps.title: Trash sweep
defaultSteps.action: Grab obvious trash and remove to bin.
cues: If in doubt, trash goes now.

defaultSteps.title: Dish & cup sweep
defaultSteps.action: Collect dishes/cups and move to kitchen sink area.

defaultSteps.title: Item roundup
defaultSteps.action: Pick up items that don't belong and place in a 'return basket'.

defaultSteps.title: Surface quick-wipe
defaultSteps.action: Quick wipe one main surface (table/counter) with dry or lightly damp microfiber.

defaultSteps.title: Floor quick-pass
defaultSteps.action: Quick visible crumbs/dust pick-up (hand vac or quick sweep).

addSteps: Do one extra surface + quick straighten fabrics/pillows

notes: Use vinegar solution for degreasy areas; use castile solution for gentle general wipe.
Do not use vinegar on natural stone.

defaultSteps.title: Choose solution
defaultSteps.action: Select vinegar spray OR castile spray based on surface and soil.
checks: Surface is NOT natural stone if using vinegar
defaultSteps.notes: Vinegar: 1:1 white vinegar + water (optional: citrus peel infusion, strain) | Castile: 1 tsp castile soap + 2 cups water (shake gently)

defaultSteps.title: Dry dust first
defaultSteps.action: Dry dust/crumb removal before wet wiping.

defaultSteps.title: Spray & dwell
defaultSteps.action: Lightly mist cloth or surface; allow brief dwell.

defaultSteps.title: Wipe in one direction
defaultSteps.action: Wipe top-to-bottom or S-pattern; flip cloth often.
cues: Fold cloth into quarters; rotate faces.

defaultSteps.title: Detail edges
defaultSteps.action: Use brush/toothbrush for seams, grout lines, knobs.

defaultSteps.title: Buff dry
defaultSteps.action: Buff with dry cloth for a clean finish.

culturalNotes.note: Citrus peel steeped in vinegar is a common traditional deodorizing cleaner; strain before use.

methods.name: Kitchen Degrease (Natural: Soap + Warm Water + Baking Soda Paste)

notes: Avoid abrasive pads on stainless/painted surfaces. Rinse residue to prevent haze.

defaultSteps.title: Pre-wipe
defaultSteps.action: Remove loose crumbs and dust.

defaultSteps.title: Soap wash
defaultSteps.action: Wipe with warm soapy water to loosen oils.

defaultSteps.title: Baking soda paste (spots)
defaultSteps.action: Apply paste (baking soda + a little water) to sticky spots.

defaultSteps.title: Gentle scrub
defaultSteps.action: Scrub lightly; use brush on crevices.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse & dry
defaultSteps.action: Wipe with clean water; dry to finish.

doneLooksLike: Stovetop is free of oily sheen
qualityChecks: Wipe with dry finger: no sticky feel

methods.name: Bathroom Soap Scum Breaker (Vinegar + Dish Soap)

notes: Do NOT use on natural stone tile. Ventilate. Rinse well.

defaultSteps.title: Mix solution
defaultSteps.action: Combine 1:1 warm vinegar + dish soap in spray bottle.

defaultSteps.title: Spray & dwell
defaultSteps.action: Spray soap scum areas; allow dwell.

defaultSteps.title: Scrub
defaultSteps.action: Scrub with non-scratch sponge; toothbrush for corners.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse hot
defaultSteps.action: Rinse with hot water until slick feel is gone.

defaultSteps.title: Squeegee & dry
defaultSteps.action: Squeegee glass/tile; dry fixtures to prevent spots.

culturalNotes.tradition: Water-squeegee daily habit (common in many households)
culturalNotes.note: A quick squeegee after bathing reduces soap scum buildup and deep-clean time.

notes: Use dedicated cloths. Wash hands after. Follow product dwell times if disinfecting.

defaultSteps.title: Prep
defaultSteps.action: Put on gloves; flush once to wet bowl.

defaultSteps.title: Apply cleaner
defaultSteps.action: Squirt soap/cleaner under rim; coat bowl.

defaultSteps.title: Brush & dwell
defaultSteps.action: Scrub bowl thoroughly; let sit.

defaultSteps.title: Wipe exterior
defaultSteps.action: Wipe tank→handle→seat top/bottom→base with damp cloth.

defaultSteps.title: Disinfect touchpoints (optional)
defaultSteps.action: If needed, apply disinfectant to handle/seat; keep wet per label then wipe.
defaultSteps.notes: Use ONE disinfectant method; never mix.

defaultSteps.title: Final flush
defaultSteps.action: Flush; rinse brush; store to dry.
cues: Brush drips stop before putting away.

notesAdd: Use baking soda sprinkle for extra scrub; follow with vinegar flush (separately) if desired.

notes: Avoid direct sun to reduce streaking. Use minimal liquid.

defaultSteps.title: Dust first
defaultSteps.action: Dry dust edges and frames.

defaultSteps.title: Mist cloth
defaultSteps.action: Mist the cloth (not the glass) to avoid drips.

defaultSteps.title: S-pattern wipe
defaultSteps.action: Wipe in S-pattern; flip cloth for final buff.
cues: Use a clean, dry side at the end.

defaultSteps.title: Squeegee (optional)
defaultSteps.action: For large glass, squeegee top-to-bottom; wipe blade each pass.

culturalNotes.note: Some households use newspaper for glass; modern inks can transfer. Microfiber is safer.

notes: Do not over-wet wood/laminate. Avoid vinegar on stone. Rinse if hazy.

defaultSteps.title: Dry clean
defaultSteps.action: Sweep/vac thoroughly, especially edges.

defaultSteps.title: Mix mop water
defaultSteps.action: Warm water + a small amount of castile/dish soap.

defaultSteps.title: Mop in sections
defaultSteps.action: Mop from far corner to exit; rinse/wring often.
cues: Water should be damp, not dripping.

defaultSteps.title: Spot scrub
defaultSteps.action: Use cloth for sticky spots; avoid scraping floors.

defaultSteps.title: Dry/air
defaultSteps.action: Allow to dry; open windows if possible.

qualityChecks: No footprints in haze after drying

notesAdd: Check manufacturer guidance; avoid on unsealed wood/laminate if not recommended.

optional: essential oil (1–2 drops per cup, optional)

notes: Keep powder away from long-pile if it clogs vacuum; test first.

defaultSteps.title: Pre-vac
defaultSteps.action: Vacuum to remove grit.

defaultSteps.title: Sprinkle
defaultSteps.action: Lightly sprinkle baking soda evenly.

defaultSteps.title: Dwell
defaultSteps.action: Let rest to absorb odors.

defaultSteps.title: Vacuum again
defaultSteps.action: Vacuum thoroughly with slow passes.

notes: Use on towels/athletic wear. Rinse thoroughly. Avoid delicate fabrics.

defaultSteps.title: Fill hot water
defaultSteps.action: Fill tub with hot water suitable for fabric.

defaultSteps.title: Add agents
defaultSteps.action: Add detergent + washing soda; optional borax/oxygen bleach.
defaultSteps.notes: Stir to dissolve before adding laundry.

defaultSteps.title: Soak
defaultSteps.action: Add items and soak, stirring occasionally.

defaultSteps.title: Drain & rinse
defaultSteps.action: Drain; rinse items until water runs clearer.

defaultSteps.title: Wash cycle
defaultSteps.action: Run normal wash (no extra detergent) then dry.
cues: Dry fully to prevent musty smell.

culturalNotes.note: Many cultures use periodic soaking/boiling of linens for freshness; modern stripping adapts this approach.

notes: Only for white cotton/linen that can handle heat. Risk of shrinkage/damage. Use dedicated pot if possible.

defaultSteps.title: Check fabric
defaultSteps.action: Confirm fabric can tolerate boiling (cotton/linen whites).

defaultSteps.title: Simmer not rolling boil
defaultSteps.action: Heat water to simmer; add detergent.
cues: Avoid aggressive boil to reduce wear.

defaultSteps.title: Add linens carefully
defaultSteps.action: Submerge with tongs; stir gently.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse & launder
defaultSteps.action: Rinse; launder normally; sun-dry if possible.

culturalNotes.note: Historically used when machine washing was unavailable; now optional for specific needs.

notes: Ensure fire safety. Some households avoid smoke due to asthma—use ventilation-only variant if needed.

defaultSteps.title: Ventilate
defaultSteps.action: Open a window/door slightly to create airflow.

defaultSteps.title: Prepare safely
defaultSteps.action: Use fire-safe dish; keep away from curtains.

defaultSteps.title: Light & walk-through
defaultSteps.action: If used in your tradition, walk through rooms briefly.
cues: Stay mindful; do not leave unattended.

defaultSteps.title: Extinguish fully
defaultSteps.action: Extinguish; confirm no embers.

defaultSteps.title: Fresh air finish
defaultSteps.action: Leave windows open a few minutes.

culturalNotes.note: Included for catalog completeness; SSA can treat this as an optional household preference method.

notes: Choose ONE disinfectant. Keep chemicals away from kids/pets.

defaultSteps.title: Shoes off zone
defaultSteps.action: Set shoes on tray; keep walkway clear.

defaultSteps.title: Wipe high-touch
defaultSteps.action: Wipe handles, switches, railings near entry.
cues: Keep surface visibly wet if disinfecting.

defaultSteps.title: Floor spot clean
defaultSteps.action: Spot mop entry floor for grit/salt.

defaultSteps.title: Hand wash
defaultSteps.action: Wash hands 20 seconds.

notes: Rinse well. Peroxide can lighten some surfaces—spot test.

defaultSteps.title: Clear & rinse
defaultSteps.action: Remove dishes and rinse sink.

defaultSteps.title: Scrub
defaultSteps.action: Use dish soap + baking soda to scrub basin and drain area.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse
defaultSteps.action: Rinse thoroughly.

defaultSteps.title: Sanitize dwell
defaultSteps.action: Spray 3% peroxide; keep wet then wipe/rinse.

defaultSteps.title: Polish
defaultSteps.action: Dry and polish faucet with dry cloth.

notes: Avoid strong fragrances. Do not spray cleaners directly on food.

defaultSteps.title: Empty one shelf
defaultSteps.action: Remove items from one shelf/drawer at a time.
cues: Work in sections to keep food cold.

defaultSteps.title: Wipe shelf
defaultSteps.action: Wipe with soapy water; then clean water.

defaultSteps.title: Dry
defaultSteps.action: Dry shelf before returning items.

defaultSteps.title: Purge expired
defaultSteps.action: Check dates; discard spoiled items.

defaultSteps.title: Door seals
defaultSteps.action: Wipe gaskets with damp cloth.

methods.name: Oven Clean (Natural: Baking Soda Paste + Vinegar Rinse)

notes: Do not use on self-clean coatings if manufacturer warns. Avoid heating vinegar fumes.

defaultSteps.title: Cool & prep
defaultSteps.action: Ensure oven is off and cool. Remove racks.

defaultSteps.title: Apply paste
defaultSteps.action: Apply thick baking soda paste to interior (avoid heating elements).

defaultSteps.title: Dwell overnight
defaultSteps.action: Let paste sit to soften grime.

defaultSteps.title: Scrape & wipe
defaultSteps.action: Gently scrape softened grime; wipe with damp cloth.

defaultSteps.title: Vinegar rinse
defaultSteps.action: Spray vinegar lightly to dissolve remaining baking soda; wipe clean.

defaultSteps.title: Racks
defaultSteps.action: Soak racks in hot soapy water; scrub and dry.

methods.name: Wood Surfaces (Gentle Damp Wipe + Dry Buff)

notes: Avoid soaking. Follow grain. Cutting boards: sanitize separately.

defaultSteps.title: Dust
defaultSteps.action: Dry dust first.

defaultSteps.title: Damp wipe
defaultSteps.action: Lightly damp cloth; wipe with the grain.

defaultSteps.title: Dry buff
defaultSteps.action: Immediately dry buff to prevent water marks.

notes: For wood boards: avoid long soaking. Rinse and dry upright.

defaultSteps.title: Salt scrub
defaultSteps.action: Sprinkle salt; scrub with half lemon.

defaultSteps.title: Rest
defaultSteps.action: Let sit briefly.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse & dry
defaultSteps.action: Rinse; dry upright.

defaultSteps.title: Oil (optional)
defaultSteps.action: Apply mineral oil thinly; wipe excess.

culturalNotes.note: Common traditional method for deodorizing cutting surfaces.

notes: Always follow the grain. Use minimal oil to avoid smears.

defaultSteps.title: Clean
defaultSteps.action: Wipe with mild soapy water; rinse cloth and wipe again.

defaultSteps.title: Dry fully
defaultSteps.action: Dry with clean cloth.

defaultSteps.title: Polish
defaultSteps.action: Add a drop of oil to cloth; buff with the grain.

notes: Beware hot steam. Let stand before opening.

defaultSteps.title: Prepare bowl
defaultSteps.action: Add water; optional lemon/vinegar.

defaultSteps.title: Heat
defaultSteps.action: Microwave until steaming.

defaultSteps.title: Rest
defaultSteps.action: Let sit closed to loosen grime.

defaultSteps.title: Wipe
defaultSteps.action: Carefully remove bowl; wipe interior.

notes: Turn off HVAC when dusting vents if desired.

defaultSteps.title: Vacuum
defaultSteps.action: Vacuum baseboards/vents with brush attachment.

defaultSteps.title: Dry wipe
defaultSteps.action: Wipe with dry microfiber.

defaultSteps.title: Damp finish
defaultSteps.action: Lightly damp wipe for stuck grime.

notes: Avoid soaking wood windows. Dry well.

defaultSteps.title: Dry loosen
defaultSteps.action: Brush grit loose.

defaultSteps.title: Vacuum
defaultSteps.action: Vacuum loosened debris.

defaultSteps.title: Wipe
defaultSteps.action: Wipe with soapy cloth; then clean water; dry.

notes: Spot clean stains separately; don't soak foam. Allow full dry.

defaultSteps.title: Strip bed
defaultSteps.action: Remove bedding; wash if needed.

defaultSteps.title: Vacuum
defaultSteps.action: Vacuum mattress thoroughly.

defaultSteps.title: Deodorize
defaultSteps.action: Sprinkle baking soda; let rest.

defaultSteps.title: Vacuum again
defaultSteps.action: Vacuum off powder.

notes: Blot; don't rub. Test first. Avoid over-wetting.

defaultSteps.title: Blot
defaultSteps.action: Blot excess spill immediately.

defaultSteps.title: Apply solution
defaultSteps.action: Lightly apply soap solution to cloth; dab stain.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse dab
defaultSteps.action: Dab with clean water cloth to remove soap.

defaultSteps.title: Dry
defaultSteps.action: Blot dry; air dry.

notes: A 3-step system improves speed and sanitation. Air-dry when possible.

defaultSteps.title: Set basins
defaultSteps.action: Basin 1 wash (hot soapy), Basin 2 rinse, Basin 3 sanitize (optional).

defaultSteps.title: Scrape & stack
defaultSteps.action: Scrape food; stack by type.

defaultSteps.title: Wash
defaultSteps.action: Wash in soapy basin.

defaultSteps.title: Rinse
defaultSteps.action: Quick rinse in clean water.

defaultSteps.title: Sanitize (optional)
defaultSteps.action: Dip per sanitizer label then air-dry.

defaultSteps.title: Air dry
defaultSteps.action: Rack dry; towel-dry only if needed.

culturalNotes.note: Common in food-service and many traditional household workflows.

notes: Included as a workflow enhancer; pair with any physical method above.

defaultSteps.title: Choose rhythm
defaultSteps.action: Pick a 10–20 minute song set or timer.

defaultSteps.title: Assign roles
defaultSteps.action: Assign tasks by beat (trash, surfaces, floors).

defaultSteps.title: Finish together
defaultSteps.action: End with a 60-second 'final pass' and celebrate completion.

culturalNotes.note: Music-driven cleaning is widely used to keep pace and reduce fatigue.
